revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !Name !Image !Type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji ware]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !Name !Image !Type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji ware]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !Name !image !Type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji ware]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]]

Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !Type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !Type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !Type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji ware]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !Type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji ware]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered burial mounds and remains of medieval residences. This group of burial mounds consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji ware]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}}

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji ware]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji ware |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji ware |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}}

Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
