{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]]{{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] {{citation needed|Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length

This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[grave goods]] been recovered from the [[burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uda, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]]

3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65282550]] Wikidata name: Mita-Osawa Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg; File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.48097222222222|N|135.9742777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Uda|qid=Q863747}}| region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:ita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}}

File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.48097222222222|N|135.9742777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Uda|WD=Q863747}}| region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side

3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group]][[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]][[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65282550]] Wikidata name: Mita-Osawa Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | native_name = 見田・大沢古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg; File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1,2,3.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mita-Ōsawa Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.48097222222222|N|135.9742777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Uda|WD=Q863747}}| region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

{{Coord|34.48097222222222|N|135.9742777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Uda|WD=Q863747}}| region = | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|51|N|135|58|27|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster'''|見田・大沢古墳群| Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun}} is a group of early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mita and Ōsawa neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2041 |title= 見田・大沢古墳群 |trans-title= Mita-Ōsawa Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge at an elevation of about 360 to 370 meters in the Uda Basin, southeast of the Nara Basin. [[Archaeological excavation]]s in 1980 discovered Kofuns and remains of medieval residences. This group of Kofuns consists of one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, consisting of two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above, and four square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumuli, each separated by a moat. Kofun No. 1, 2, and 3 are built on the ridge that runs north to south, and Kofun No. 4 and 5 are built on the ridge that runs east to west. Many [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] been recovered from the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of each of these tombs, and indicate that the construction period was at the start of Kofun period, at a time when the large ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumuli were starting to appear in the Nara Basin.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster !name !image !type !Dimensions !burial facility !Grave goods |- |'''Kofun No.1'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-1.jpg|100px]]||keyhole-shaped||27.5 meter length
20 meters diameter circular portion
12.7 meters wide rectangular portion||details unknown||style="text-align:left"| |- |'''Kofun No.2'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-2.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
1.7 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], amber ''[[magatama]]'', small glass beads, chlorite schist beads, hatchet, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.3'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-3.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||16 meters per side
3 meters high||Unknown due to grave robbing||style="text-align:left"|iron swords, arrowheads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |- |'''Kofun No.4'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-4.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||17meters per side
3 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Four-beast mirror, magatama, cylindrical beads, iron sword, Haji pottery |- |'''Kofun No.5'''||[[File:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group-5.jpg|100px]]|| Square ||14 meters per side
3.2 meters high||Direct burial in split bamboo coffin||style="text-align:left"|Haji pottery |} The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.uda.nara.jp/bunkazai/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazai/mita-osawakohungun.html Uda City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01204.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Guide] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uda, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[en:Mita-Ōsawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mita-Osawa Kofun Group}} [[ja:見田・大沢古墳群]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q65282550}}
